Transitioning from the theoretical importance of SOPs to their actual 
creation and deployment requires a rigorous, systematic 
methodology. The architecture of a perfect Standard Operating 
Procedure is a delicate balance between comprehensive detail and 
pragmatic usability. If an SOP is too vague, it fails to standardize the 
process; if it is overly pedantic and dense, it creates an impenetrable 
wall of text that employees will actively avoid. Therefore, the

architectural design of an SOP must begin with a clear understanding 
of its structural components. A standardized template is crucial across 
the organization. This template typically begins with the Title and 
Purpose, articulating exactly why the procedure exists and the specific 
outcome it aims to achiev e. This is followed by the Scope, which 
delineates the boundaries of the SOP —who it applies to, what

a common lexic on, preventing misunderstandings arising from

The core of the SOP is the step -by-step instructional sequence. The 
formatting of this section must be tailored to the complexity of the 
task and the demographic of the end -user. For relatively simple, 
sequential tasks, a Simple Steps format (a numbered li st) is sufficient.

[git] •
Model-based vs. instance based learning
Supervised learning
Informal Definition
In supervised learning, the training data you feed to the algorithm includes
the desired solutions, calledlabels.
Formal Definition
In the predictive or supervised learning approach, the goal is to learn a
mapping ˆM from inputs ⃗ x∈ RD to outputs y ∈ Y, given a labeled set of
input-output pairs D = {(⃗ xi, yi)}N
i=1 in order to predicty from a given⃗ x:
y = ˆM⃗ α(⃗ x|D, ⃗θ) + ϵ.

Model-based learning

predictions.

Main challenges of machine learning
Poor-quality data
If the training data is full of errors, outliers, and noise, it will make it harder
for the system to detect the underlying patterns.
If some instances are clearly outliers, it may help to simply discard them or
try to fix the errors manually.
If some instances are missing a few features, you must decide
whether you want to ignore this attribute (column of feature matrix)
altogether,
ignore these instances (row of feature matrix),

[git] •
Main challenges of machine learning
Irrelevant features
Garbage in, garbage out. A critical part of the success of a Machine
Learning project is coming up with a good set of features to train on. This
process, calledfeature engineering, involves:
Feature selection selecting the most useful features to train on among
existing features.
Feature extraction combining existing features to produce a more useful
one.
Gathering new data.

[git] •
Main challenges of machine learning
Underfitting the data
Underfitting is the opposite of overfitting: it occurs when your model is too
simple to learn the underlying structure of the data.
The main options to fix this problem are:
Selecting a more powerful model, with more parameters
Feeding better features to the learning algorithm (feature engineering)
Reducing the constraints on the model (e.g., reducing the
regularization hyperparameter)
